title: Stenotyla lankesteriana
intro: Stenotyla lankesteriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as a type of vine or climber. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stenotyla lankesteriana include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Stenotyla lankesteriana is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification reflects its close relationship with other orchid species. Further, it is placed within the subfamily Cymbidioideae, which is a distinct subgroup within the orchid family. Within the Cymbidioideae subfamily, Stenotyla lankesteriana is assigned to the tribe Zygopetalinae, a group characterized by unique floral and reproductive structures. The exact classification of Stenotyla lankesteriana is still subject to ongoing botanical research.
